title: Your Business Associate Agreement, explained.
intro: Before we access your records, we sign a BAA with your practice. This page explains what that document is, why it protects you, and what we commit to under it — in plain English. When you’re ready, request a copy of ours.

glance.title: It’s required by HIPAA
glance.body: Any partner that handles PHI on a provider’s behalf must have a signed BAA in place. It’s the law, not a formality.

glance.title: It protects your patients
glance.body: It binds us to specific safeguards and makes us directly liable for protecting the information you entrust to us.

glance.title: We sign before day one
glance.body: Our standard onboarding process requires a signed BAA before PHI access.

whyWhen.title: Why the law requires it
whyWhen.body: HIPAA holds providers responsible for the PHI they share with partners. A BAA passes specific, enforceable obligations down to that partner — so protection follows the data.

whyWhen.title: Why it protects you
whyWhen.body: Without a signed BAA, sharing PHI with us would itself be a HIPAA violation. With one, your practice is covered and we carry direct, documented accountability.

whyWhen.title: When it gets signed
whyWhen.body: During onboarding, before any systems access or PHI exposure. Our standard onboarding sequence is: BAA executed, systems secured, SOPs set — then work begins.

ourResp: Use and disclose PHI only as the agreement and HIPAA permit — never for marketing.
Apply administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to all PHI we handle.
Limit access to the minimum necessary, on a least-privilege basis.
Ensure any subcontractor that touches PHI signs an equivalent agreement.
Report security incidents and breaches to you promptly, with the facts.
Make our practices available for audit, and return or destroy PHI at the end of the engagement.
clientResp: Provide PHI only through the secure channels we establish together.
Share the minimum information we need to perform the agreed services.
Tell us about any restrictions a patient has requested on their information.
Keep your own Notice of Privacy Practices and patient consents current.

commitments.title: Security commitments
commitments.body: Encryption in transit and at rest, MFA and VPN on login, company-managed devices, and audit logging — written into the agreement, not just promised.

commitments.title: Incident reporting
commitments.body: We commit to prompt notification, consistent with your BAA, if a security incident or breach involving your PHI occurs — with a clear remediation plan.

commitments.title: Subcontractors
commitments.body: A tool or partner that may access PHI — including AI vendors — is required to sign its own BAA before use, so the chain of accountability is maintained.

commitments.title: Compliance & audit
commitments.body: We keep records for six years, review controls regularly, and make our compliance practices available so your team can verify, not just trust.

faqs.q: Do we have to use your BAA, or can we use ours?
faqs.a: Either works. We have a standard BAA ready for your compliance team to review, and we’re equally happy to sign yours. What matters is that a mutually agreed agreement is fully executed before any PHI changes hands.

faqs.q: When exactly is the BAA signed in the process?
faqs.a: During onboarding, before systems access or PHI exposure. Our standard sequence is BAA executed first, then systems secured, then SOPs and access configured. Your team never works on live PHI before the agreement is in place.

faqs.q: What counts as PHI in our engagement?
faqs.a: Any individually identifiable health information we encounter while supporting you — patient names and contact details, insurance and claims data, appointment and scheduling records, and clinical documentation. We use only the minimum necessary for each task.

faqs.q: How do you handle AI tools under the BAA?
faqs.a: An AI tool that may process PHI is subject to its own signed BAA before we use it, and human review is required on AI-assisted clinical or billing outputs before finalization. We minimize PHI in AI prompts to the minimum necessary, and accountability for the result is always ours — never “the AI did it.”

faqs.q: What happens to our data when the engagement ends?
faqs.a: At the end of the relationship we return or securely destroy the PHI we hold on your behalf, as the agreement specifies, and we document that it was done. Required compliance and audit records are retained only as the law allows.

faqs.q: How do we request a copy?
faqs.a: Email compliance@carentix.com or use the button below, and we’ll send our standard BAA along with our safeguards documentation so your compliance officer has everything needed to review and approve.
